channel.title: After the Code, the Structure Remains on After the Code
channel.description: Recent content in After the Code, the Structure Remains on After the Code

item.title: An Audit Workflow Survives Only When It Absorbs Misses — Eight Reinforcements to sec-audit-static v2.0
item.description: I designed sec-audit-static workflow v2.0, ran it against a real auth-server codebase, and missed two things. This is the record of how those misses were folded back into the tool — through v2.8.

item.title: Security Controls Aren't Lacking — They're Inconvenient: Why Security Needs Customer Context
item.description: Security controls already exist. The real problem is that we cannot decide which customer, at which moment, deserves how much friction. As the closing chapter of the CAPTCHA·ATO series, this post is about moving from quantity of controls to context of controls — adaptive security as an operational discipline.

item.title: MCP Is Repeating the History of RPC Security
item.description: MCP security risks are not about prompt injection. They stem from the same configuration-to-execution escalation pattern that has plagued RPC, local security software, and CI/CD pipelines for decades.

item.title: Supply Chain Security Does Not End with SBOM: Governing AI Development Tools and Automation Connections
item.description: AI IDEs, MCP, and automation connectors are not merely developer convenience tools. They are supply-chain assets that affect the trust path of how code is written, reviewed, and shipped.

item.title: Security Assessment Becomes a Development Process, Not an Outsourced Event
item.description: AI-era security assessment is not primarily about reducing outsourcing cost. It is about embedding repeatable verification into the development process while separating automation candidates from human judgment.

item.title: The AI Slop Paradox: Why Triage Gets Harder When Vulnerabilities Get Easier to Find
item.description: AI lowers the cost of finding vulnerability candidates, but it also increases low-quality reports, duplicates, and false positives. In the AI slop era, triage quality becomes the core security operation.

item.title: Beyond CVE Response: AI-Era Vulnerabilities Move Before They Get Numbers
item.description: AI-era vulnerability response cannot wait for a CVE number. Pre-CVE signals such as issues, commits, PoCs, write-ups, and patch traces now have to be mapped against internal exposure earlier.

item.title: After the Code, the Structure Remains
item.description: The representative foreword of this blog: security now fails less at finding issues than at absorbing, sustaining, and acting on what has already been found.

item.title: Why Security Knowledge Transfer Fails — and What to Design Instead
item.description: An organizational design report that reframes the security–DevOps problem from failed knowledge transfer to default design, interfaces, exception handling, and alignment.

item.title: How I Turned 228 Endpoints into 5 Clusters
item.description: A practical account of applying dataflow-based clustering to a real codebase — reducing 228 endpoints to 5 reviewable clusters, and finding an RCE chain in the cross-section.

item.title: Why Account Takeover Never Ends — Dismantling the ATO Supply Chain
item.description: How Korea's CaaS supply chain reproduces itself through the recycling loop of points, gift cards, and crypto — and why defending against it requires reading the entire behavioral network, not just the login page.

item.title: Structure Builders Will Outlast Vulnerability Finders
item.description: 18 years of vulnerability hunting distilled into one insight: the shift from individual instinct to scalable structure — and what AI means for those left standing.

item.title: The CAPTCHA That Became a Free Automatic Door for Hackers — A Bypass PoC and Defense Strategy
item.description: A practical look at an audio CAPTCHA bypass PoC built with Playwright, Whisper, and Page-Agent, plus the login defenses that still matter after CAPTCHA falls.

item.title: Security Diagnostics Reports Die Upon Publication
item.description: We point out the limitations of traditional security diagnostic reports and share the necessity and practical application cases of 'Security Testing as Code', managing diagnostic results not as 'documents' but as 'executable code (PoC)'.

item.title: WAF/IPS/IDS Detection Gap Analysis and Remediation Direction
item.description: Structural analysis of WAF, IPS, and IDS detection gaps from parsing discrepancies, with a practical remediation taxonomy.

item.title: Contracts vs Security Governance — Contracts Enforce. Governance Decides.
item.description: Why security governance must drive decisions before contracts enforce them—a structural reframing for security leadership.

item.title: eIDAS 2.0 vs. Korea’s Digital Identity System: A Comparative Analysis
item.description: Comparative analysis of EU eIDAS 2.0 wallet-based identity and Korea's mobile ID system across governance, privacy, and operations.

item.title: Amadey Malware: A Comparative Study of Static Detection vs Memory-Based Detection
item.description: Comparing static vs memory-based malware detection using Amadey, showing why runtime YARA rules outperform file-hash approaches.

item.title: The Visibility Principle: How Internal Vulnerability Visibility Shapes Remediation Behavior
item.description: How transparent internal vulnerability visibility drives remediation through accountability and deterrence without formal punishment.

item.title: Attack Surface Management in 2025: Why Continuous Visibility is Essential
item.description: Why continuous attack surface management is critical in 2025, covering AI-driven discovery, shadow IT, and zero trust integration.

item.title: The Gap Between CISO Strategy and Execution: The WAF Debate and Field Leadership Report
item.description: A comprehensive report presenting a roadmap for practical security improvement and field leadership, centered around the debate on WAF and the gap between philosophy and execution.

item.title: The Limitations of 'Secure' SSRF Patches: Advanced Bypasses and Defense-in-Depth
item.description: A deep dive into why common SSRF defense code is often incomplete, real-world bypasses, and practical, layered mitigation strategies for developers and security engineers.

item.title: Exception Serialization Patterns in OpenStack Nova: Theoretical RCE Risks and Lessons Learned
item.description: Theoretical RCE risks in OpenStack Nova's exception serialization via oslo.messaging, with PoC scenarios and defense patterns.

item.title: Endpoint Security Evasion (2020–2025): From EDR Bypass to EDR Kill
item.description: A technical analysis of how BYOI, BYOVD, DLL hijacking, and service abuse shifted endpoint attacks from EDR bypass to EDR kill between 2020 and 2025.

item.title: SPOF in Cybersecurity: From History to Strategy, a Graph-Based Analysis
item.description: Graph-based analysis of Single Points of Failure in cybersecurity, using weighted path enumeration to identify critical infrastructure nodes.

item.title: Dicer Module Vulnerability Mitigation Guide: CVE-2022-24434
item.description: Mitigation guide for CVE-2022-24434 in the Dicer module affecting Multer and Express, with practical dependency chain fixes.

item.title: How I Managed Unmaintained Open Source with Gmail and Snyk Alerts
item.description: Automating Snyk vulnerability alert management with Google Apps Script and Gmail when official API access falls short.

item.title: Human Insight and Artificial Intelligence: Dialogue at an Impossible Crossroads
item.description: Can AI achieve enlightenment? Exploring the asymmetry between human insight and machine repetition, with technical and philosophical limits.

item.title: Detection Frameworks and Latest Methodologies for eBPF-Based Backdoors
item.description: How eBPF-based backdoors evade traditional detection, and modern frameworks like Tracee and LKRG that counter kernel-level threats.

item.title: In-Depth Report on Telecommunication Security: SKT Breach and Global Case Studies
item.description: In-depth analysis of the 2025 SKT breach, telecom authentication mechanisms, and 5G SA vs NSA security architecture differences.

item.title: CVE-2019-17570 Apache XML-RPC Vulnerability Analysis Report
item.description: Technical analysis of CVE-2019-17570 deserialization RCE in Apache XML-RPC, with patch comparison and secure implementation guidance.

item.title: Is Your Data in the Cat's Paws?
item.description: Analysis of the 2025 KakaoPay breach exposing 40M users' data, and why formal consent fails without AI-based DPIA and civic oversight.

item.title: There’s No Such Thing as a Free Lunch, But Security Was Free
item.description: The CVE system nearly collapsed in 2025. Who should fund public cybersecurity infrastructure when free-riding is no longer sustainable?

item.title: In the AI Era, Employees Are Isolated and Organizations Thrive
item.description: A satirical critique of how AI-era organizations isolate employees through async workflows and data-driven control to suppress solidarity.

item.title: The Place of Humans: Declaring the Creator’s Rights in the Age of AI
item.description: A philosophical essay on human authorship and creativity in the AI era, declaring the creator's rights over machine-generated output.

item.title: Common Security Myths Developers Tell Themselves
item.description: Debunking developer security myths around responsibility deflection, tech overconfidence, and risk underestimation with real-world examples.

item.title: How to Block ECH and Mitigate DoH in Enterprise Networks
item.description: A hands-on guide using dnsmasq to filter SVCB and HTTPS records for disabling ECH and enforcing central DNS policies. Notes that DoH requires separate network-layer policies.

item.title: XML-RPC Security Vulnerabilities Analysis and Mitigation Strategies
item.description: Comprehensive analysis of XML-RPC security flaws including RCE, XXE, and DDoS, with mitigation strategies and a Python PoC exploit.

item.title: Review of Citrix Security Policy Effectiveness
item.description: Citrix administrators apply security policies to each user’s VDI (Virtual Desktop Infrastructure) through Citrix Group Policy. However, certain structural vulnerabilities in Citrix CSE (Citrix Service Engine) and the Citrix VDI Agent allow for potential bypassing of these security policies.

item.title: KPIs Can Cause Incidents!!!
item.description: Real cases where manipulated KPI metrics led to cybersecurity incidents, exposing the dangers of metric-driven security management.

item.title: Common Misconceptions of Security Assessors
item.description: Three common misconceptions that weaken security assessments and strategies to build repeatable, effective vulnerability evaluation.

item.title: Can Development Culture Influence Security Levels?
item.description: Exploring how development culture shapes code security, with practical examples using static analysis tools like Joern.

item.title: Bypassing citrix policy is not a vulnerability, but it can be a violation of the law
item.description: How Citrix VDI policies can be bypassed via registry manipulation—a legal risk analysis with detection and mitigation strategies.

item.title: Strengthening cybersecurity through government ngos and bug bounty programs
item.description: How government NGOs and bug bounty programs strengthen cybersecurity, with insights on security taxes and public-private collaboration.

item.title: Security threats and mitigation strategies for java reflection
item.description: The **Java Reflection API** is a powerful tool that allows dynamic manipulation of classes, methods, and interfaces at runtime. However, due to its flexibility, it introduces significant security risks, as attackers can exploit it to gain unauthorized access to systems. In this article, we will explore the security threats posed by Java Reflection and outline strategies to mitigate these risks.

item.title: About the XSSAudit
item.description: Why Google removed XSSAudit from Chrome and what it means for web security—analyzing the shift from XSS filters to CSP.

item.title: About
item.description: This site tracks how security findings become durable change across code, method, and governance.
